sections.title: Solution Overview
sections.description: What is being built and why agentic AI is the right approach.
required_content: One-paragraph summary of the process being agentified
Why agentic AI vs RPA or traditional automation
Core value proposition — what changes for the business
Key assumptions and constraints
kb_queries: current state pain points manual steps

sections.title: Agent Definitions
sections.description: Each agent in the solution: name, responsibility, tools, inputs, outputs, decision authority.
required_content: Table of agents: name, responsibility, Strands tools used, inputs, outputs
What each agent decides autonomously vs escalates
handler() function signature for each agent (Arbiter pattern)
Agent registration config: agentId, description, input schema

sections.title: Orchestration & Data Flow
sections.description: How agents coordinate, sequencing, data passed between agents, failure handling.
required_content: Step-by-step orchestration flow for the primary happy path
Data passed between agents — field names and types
Exception handling and retry strategy
Concurrency considerations

sections.title: Integrations
sections.description: External systems each agent connects to: how, auth, key data exchanged.
required_content: Table: system, agent that uses it, integration method (http_request/use_aws/custom tool), auth method
Key request/response fields for each integration
Custom tools needed for integrations not covered by Strands built-ins
Rate limits, error codes, retry behaviour

sections.title: Human-in-the-Loop Design
sections.description: Where humans stay in the loop: triggers, routing, SLAs, override mechanisms.
required_content: Table: trigger condition, who is notified, SLA, action required
How approval responses are routed back into the orchestration
Audit trail — what is logged and where
Compliance controls mapped to specific agent behaviours
kb_queries: approval thresholds human review compliance requirements
